title: TurboQuant: Redefining AI efficiency with extreme compression
content: TurboQuant: Redefining AI efficiency with extreme compression TurboQuant: Redefining AI efficiency with extreme compression March 24, 2026 Amir Zandieh, Research Scientist, and Vahab Mirrokni, VP and Google Fellow, Google Research We introduce a set of advanced theoretically grounded quantization algorithms that enable massive compression for large language models and vector search engines. Quick links TurboQuant Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss PolarQuant Share Copy link × Vectors are the fundamental way AI models understand and process information. Small vectors describe simple attributes, such as a point in a graph, while “high-dimensional” vectors capture complex information such as the features of an image, the meaning of a word, or the properties of a dataset. High-dimensional vectors are incredibly powerful, but they also consume vast amounts of memory, leading to bottlenecks in the key-value cache , a high-speed "digital cheat sheet" that stores frequently used information under simple labels so a computer can retrieve it instantly without having to search through a slow, massive database. Vector quantization is a powerful, classical data compression technique that reduces the size of high-dimensional vectors. This optimization addresses two critical facets of AI: it enhances vector search , the high-speed technology powering large-scale AI and search engines, by enabling faster similarity lookups; and it helps unclog key-value cache bottlenecks by reducing the size of key-value pairs, which enables faster similarity searches and lowers memory costs. However, traditional vector quantization usually introduces its own "memory overhead” as most methods require calculating and storing (in full precision) quantization constants for every small block of data. This overhead can add 1 or 2 extra bits per number, partially defeating the purpose of vector quantization. Today, we introduce TurboQuant (to be presented at ICLR 2026 ), a compression algorithm that optimally addresses the challenge of memory overhead in vector quantization. We also present Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss (QJL), and PolarQuant (to be presented at AISTATS 2026 ), which TurboQuant uses to achieve its results. In testing, all three techniques showed great promise for reducing key-value bottlenecks without sacrificing AI model performance. This has potentially profound implications for all compression-reliant use cases, including and especially in the domains of search and AI. How TurboQuant works TurboQuant is a compression method that achieves a high reduction in model size with zero accuracy loss, making it ideal for supporting both key-value (KV) cache compression and vector search . It accomplishes this via two key steps: High-quality compression (the PolarQuant method) : TurboQuant starts by randomly rotating the data vectors. This clever step simplifies the data's geometry, making it easy to apply a standard, high-quality quantizer (a tool that maps a large set of con
title_zh: TurboQuant：通过极致压缩重新定义AI效率
summary_zh: 谷歌研究团队于2026年3月24日发布了TurboQuant，这是一套基于先进理论的量化算法，旨在通过极致压缩技术重新定义AI效率。该技术能够对大型语言模型（LLM）和向量搜索引擎进行大规模压缩，以解决高维向量消耗大量内存导致的瓶颈问题，特别是在关键值缓存（KV cache）和向量搜索（vector search）场景中。，高维向量是AI模型理解和处理复杂信息（如图像特征、语义或数据集属性）的基础，但其庞大的内存占用会拖慢关键值缓存——这个作为高速“数字小抄”的组件，用于存储常用信息以实现快速检索。传统的向量量化技术虽能压缩向量尺寸，加速相似性查找并降低内存成本，但其自身通常会引入“内存开销”，因为大多数方法需要为每个数据块计算并存储全精度的量化常数，这可能导致每个数值额外增加1-2比特，部分抵消了压缩的效益。，TurboQuant（将于ICLR 2026发表）作为一种压缩算法，优化解决了向量量化中的内存开销挑战。它通过两个关键步骤实现模型尺寸的大幅缩减且不损失精度：首先，采用高质量压缩（PolarQuant方法），通过随机旋转数据向量来简化数据几何结构，从而应用标准的高质量量化器；其次，结合量化约翰逊-林登斯特劳斯（Quantized Johnson-Lindenstrauss, QJL）方法，进一步优化压缩过程。此外，团队还同步提出了PolarQuant（将于AISTATS 2026发表），TurboQuant利用这些技术达成其压缩目标。，测试表明，TurboQuant、QJL和PolarQuant这三种技术都能在不牺牲AI模型性能的前提下，有效缓解关键值缓存瓶颈。这对于所有依赖压缩的应用场景，尤其是搜索和AI领域，具有深远的潜在影响。TurboQuant的核心价值在于，它通过极致的压缩效率，提升了向量搜索的速度，降低了内存成本，为大规模AI部署和高效检索系统提供了新的解决方案。

title: Hypura – A storage-tier-aware LLM inference scheduler for Apple Silicon
content: GitHub - t8/hypura: Run models too big for your Mac's memory · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert t8 / hypura Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 8 Star 433 main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 44 Commits 44 Commits benches benches benchmarks benchmarks hypura-sys hypura-sys patches patches src src tests tests vendor vendor .gitignore .gitignore .gitmodules .gitmodules CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.md Cargo.lock Cargo.lock Cargo.toml Cargo.toml README.md README.md RESEARCH_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md RESEARCH_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md View all files Repository files navigation _ _ | | | |_ _ _ __ _ _ _ __ __ _ | |_| | | | | '_ \| | | | '__/ _` | | _ | |_| | |_) | |_| | | | (_| | |_| |_|\__, | .__/ \__,_|_| \__,_| |___/|_| Run models too big for your Mac's memory Hypura is a storage-tier-aware LLM inference scheduler for Apple Silicon. It places model tensors across GPU, RAM, and NVMe tiers based on access patterns, bandwidth costs, and hardware capabilities — enabling models that exceed physical memory to run without crashing the system. Run a 31 GB Mixtral 8x7B on a 32 GB Mac Mini at 2.2 tok/s. A 40 GB Llama 70B at 0.3 tok/s. Vanilla llama.cpp crashes on both. Why does this matter? Consumer hardware (MacBook Pro, Mac Studio) ships with fast unified memory and NVMe storage, but limited capacity. A 32 GB M1 Max cannot naively load a 40 GB model — the OS will swap-thrash until the OOM killer intervenes. Hypura solves this by understanding the model architecture: Norms and embeddings are tiny but accessed every token — pinned to GPU MoE expert routing exploits sparsity — only 2 of 8 experts fire per token. Router interception identifies selected experts in the eval callback, then loads only the needed expert strides from NVMe (75% I/O reduction). A neuron cache tracks loaded expert slices across tokens, achieving 99.5% hit rate from temporal locality. Co-activation tracking predicts which experts will fire next for speculative prefetch. Dense FFN weights (gate, up, down — ~60% of model size) stream from NVMe through a dynamically-sized pool buffer while attention + norms stay GPU-resident. Prefetch lookahead depth scales automatically with available memory. The result: models that would crash your machine under naive mmap become runnable. Models that fit in memory run at full Metal GPU speed with zero overhead. How it works Hypura reads the GGUF file, profiles your hardware (GPU working set, RAM, NVMe bandwidth), and solves a placement optimization that assigns every tensor to a tier: GPU (Metal) — Attention layers, norms, embeddings. Fastest access, limited by recommendedMaxWorkingSetSize . RAM — Over
title_zh: Hypura——专为苹果芯片设计的存储层级感知型LLM推理调度器
summary_zh: Hypura 是一款专为 Apple Silicon 设计的、具备存储层级感知能力的大语言模型（LLM）推理调度器。其核心目标是让用户能够在内存容量有限的 Mac 上运行远超物理内存大小的模型，而不会导致系统崩溃。，传统方法（如朴素的 llama.cpp）在尝试加载超过物理内存的模型时，操作系统会频繁进行内存交换，最终触发内存不足（OOM）终止进程。Hypura 通过智能地根据模型架构、访问模式和硬件能力，将模型张量动态放置于 GPU、统一内存（RAM）和 NVMe 存储这三个层级中，从而解决了这一问题。，其技术关键在于深度理解模型结构并优化数据调度：，1. 关键组件常驻 GPU：对每个 Token 都需访问的归一化层（Norms）和嵌入层（Embeddings），将其固定在 GPU 内存中以获得最快访问速度。，2. 高效处理混合专家模型（MoE）：针对如 Mixtral 8x7B 这类 MoE 模型，Hypura 利用其稀疏性。它通过路由拦截在评估回调中识别每个 Token 实际激活的专家（例如 8 个中的 2 个），并仅从 NVMe 加载所需专家的权重数据，减少了高达 75% 的 I/O。，3. 智能缓存与预取：引入神经元缓存来跨 Token 缓存已加载的专家切片，利用时间局部性实现了 99.5% 的缓存命中率。同时，通过协同激活跟踪来预测下一个可能激活的专家，进行推测性预取。，4. 动态流式处理稠密层：对于占模型体积约 60% 的稠密前馈网络（FFN）权重（gate, up, down），Hypura 让其从 NVMe 流式传输，通过一个动态大小的池缓冲区进行加载，而注意力机制等计算则持续在 GPU 上进行。预取前瞻深度会根据可用内存自动调整。，实际效果显著：在 32GB 内存的 Mac Mini 上，Hypura 可以运行 31GB 的 Mixtral 8x7B 模型，速度达到 2.2 tok/s；运行 40GB 的 Llama 70B 模型，速度为 0.3 tok/s。而对于本就能完全装入内存的模型，Hypura 则能实现完整的 Metal GPU 加速，且无额外开销。，工作原理上，Hypura 读取 GGUF 模型文件，分析硬件配置（GPU 工作集大小、RAM、NVMe 带宽），并通过求解一个放置优化问题，为每个张量分配最优的存储层级（GPU、RAM 或 NVMe），从而实现超大模型在消费级 Apple 硬件上的可行推理。

title: Show HN: AI Roundtable – Let 200 models debate your question
content: AI Roundtable | Compare AI Models on Any Question
title_zh: Show HN：AI圆桌会议——让200个模型辩论你的问题
summary_zh: AI Roundtable是一个创新的在线平台，允许用户输入任何问题，并同时提交给超过200个AI模型进行“圆桌辩论”式的回答与比较。该平台的核心功能是聚合和对比不同大型语言模型（LLM）的响应，旨在帮助用户直观地评估各模型的性能、风格和知识深度。，用户只需在统一界面中输入一个问题，平台便会将问题并行发送至包括GPT-4、Claude 3、Gemini、Llama以及众多开源模型在内的广泛模型列表。随后，所有模型的回答会以清晰、并列的格式呈现，方便用户横向对比。平台不仅显示原始文本回复，还可能提供响应时间、置信度提示等元数据。，其关键特点在于规模化和易用性。它一次性接入海量模型API，省去了用户分别访问不同服务或自行搭建测试环境的繁琐。对于开发者、研究人员或普通用户而言，这成为了一个高效的模型评测和选型工具。用户可以通过具体问题的实际输出来判断哪个模型更符合特定需求，例如创意写作、代码生成、事实问答或逻辑推理。，该项目的价值在于提供了LLM领域的“实时竞技场”，增强了模型性能的透明度和可比性。它降低了探索AI模型的门槛，使更多人能够基于实证而非营销宣传来理解当前AI的能力边界与差异。未来，此类工具对于推动模型改进、辅助应用开发以及普及AI知识都具有重要意义。

This skill keeps you current. /last30days researches your topic across Reddit, X, Bluesky, YouTube, TikTok, Instagram, Hacker News, Polymarket, and the web from the last 30 days, finds what the community is actually upvoting, sharing, betting on, and saying on camera, and writes you a grounded narrative with real citations. Whether it's Seedance 2.0 access, paper.design prompts, or the latest Nano Banana Pro techniques, you'll know what people who are paying attention already know. New in v2.9.5 — Bluesky, Comparative Mode, and Config Improvements: Bluesky/AT Protocol is now a social source. Opt-in via BSKY_HANDLE + BSKY_APP_PASSWORD (create at bsky.app/settings/app-passwords). Full pipeline: search, score, dedupe, render. Comparative mode - ask "X vs Y" (e.g., /last30 cursor vs windsurf ) and get 3 parallel research passes with a side-by-side comparison: strengths, weaknesses, head-to-head table, and a data-driven verdict. Per-project .env config - drop a .claude/last30days.env in your project root for per-project API keys. SessionStart config check - validates your config automatically when a Claude Code session starts. Expanded test coverage - 455+ tests across all modules. New in v2.9.1 — Auto-save to ~/Documents/Last30Days/: Every run now saves the complete briefing as a topic-named .md file to your Documents folder. Build a personal research library automatically. Inspired by @devin_explores . New in v2.9 — ScrapeCreators Reddit + Top C
title_zh: mvanhorn/last30days-skill - 一款AI智能体技能，可研究Reddit、X、YouTube、HN、Polymarket及全网任意主题，并生成基于事实的综合摘要
summary_zh: GitHub项目mvanhorn/last30days-skill是一个AI agent技能，旨在帮助用户快速了解过去30天内任何话题的最新动态。该技能能够跨多个平台进行调研，包括Reddit、X（原Twitter）、Bluesky、YouTube、TikTok、Instagram、Hacker News、Polymarket以及更广泛的网络内容。其核心功能是收集并分析社区在这些平台上的真实互动——例如点赞、分享、投注和视频讨论——然后生成一份基于事实、附有真实引用的综合性叙述摘要。，该项目主要作为Claude的插件运行，用户可以通过Claude Code或插件市场安装。其核心价值在于高效追踪快速变化的AI等领域的最新趋势、工具或争议话题（例如Seedance 2.0访问权限、paper.design提示词或Nano Banana Pro技术），让用户能及时掌握前沿社区关注的重点。，最新版本v2.9.5带来了多项重要更新：首先，新增了对Bluesky/AT Protocol社交平台的支持，用户可通过配置BSKY_HANDLE和BSKY_APP_PASSWORD来启用该来源。其次，引入了“对比模式”，用户可以输入“X vs Y”格式的查询（例如“/last30 cursor vs windsurf”），技能会并行执行三次调研，生成一份并排对比报告，内容包括各自的优势劣势、直接对比表格以及数据驱动的结论。此外，新增了按项目配置.env文件的功能，允许在项目根目录放置.claude/last30days.env来管理专属API密钥，并在Claude Code会话开始时自动验证配置。该版本还包含了超过455个模块测试，以提升稳定性。，此前版本v2.9.1增加了自动保存功能，每次运行都会将完整的简报以主题命名的.md文件格式保存到用户“~/Documents/Last30Days/”文件夹中，自动构建个人研究资料库。该技能通过搜索、评分、去重和渲染的完整流程，致力于提供接地气、有据可查的近期信息综述。

title_zh: BerriAI/litellm - Python SDK 和代理服务器（AI网关），支持以 OpenAI（或原生）格式调用 100 多种大语言模型 API，具备成本追踪、防护机制、负载均衡和日志记录功能。[支持 Bedrock、Azure、OpenAI、VertexAI、Cohere、Anthropic、Sagemaker、HuggingFace、VLLM、NVIDIA NIM]
summary_zh: BerriAI/litellm 是一个开源项目，提供了一个统一的 Python SDK 和代理服务器（AI Gateway），旨在简化对超过 100 种大型语言模型（LLM）API 的调用。其核心价值在于允许开发者使用标准化的 OpenAI API 格式或各厂商的原生格式，来访问来自不同供应商的模型，从而显著降低集成复杂性和维护成本。，该项目支持的主流 LLM 提供商和平台包括 Bedrock、Azure OpenAI、OpenAI、Google Vertex AI、Cohere、Anthropic（Claude）、Amazon SageMaker、Hugging Face、vLLM 以及 NVIDIA NIM 等。通过 LiteLLM，开发者可以用一套统一的代码与所有这些后端服务进行交互。，LiteLLM 的核心功能与特点包括：，1. 标准化接口：将所有支持的 LLM API 封装成一致的 OpenAI 格式端点，例如 `/chat/completions`、`/embeddings`、`/images`、`/audio` 等，极大简化了开发流程。，2. 企业级代理服务器（AI Gateway）：该项目包含一个功能强大的代理服务器，可作为集中式的 AI 网关。它提供了生产环境所需的关键功能，如成本跟踪、防护栏（guardrails）、负载均衡和全面的日志记录。，3. 成本与运营管理：内置的成本跟踪功能帮助团队监控不同模型的使用开销。负载均衡能力可以优化多个模型或 API 密钥间的请求分配，提升可靠性和性能。，4. 灵活的部署选项：项目提供了 Docker 配置、详细的文档和示例（cookbook），支持从本地开发到云端的多种部署方式。此外，还提供托管代理服务和企业级支持选项。，总之，LiteLLM 通过提供一个抽象层和功能丰富的网关，解决了多 LLM 供应商环境下的碎片化问题，使开发者能够更高效、更可控地构建和运营基于大语言模型的应用程序。

Node for Offline Media, Archives, and Data Knowledge That Never Goes Offline Project N.O.M.A.D. is a self-contained, offline-first knowledge and education server packed with critical tools, knowledge, and AI to keep you informed and empowered—anytime, anywhere. Installation & Quickstart Project N.O.M.A.D. can be installed on any Debian-based operating system (we recommend Ubuntu). Installation is completely terminal-based, and all tools and resources are designed to be accessed through the browser, so there's no need for a desktop environment if you'd rather setup N.O.M.A.D. as a "server" and access it through other clients. Note: sudo/root privileges are required to run the install script Quick Install (Debian-based OS Only) sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y curl && curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Crosstalk-Solutions/project-nomad/refs/heads/main/install/install_nomad.sh -o install_nomad.sh && sudo bash install_nomad.sh Project N.O.M.A.D. is now installed on your device! Open a browser and navigate to http://localhost:8080 (or http://DEVICE_IP:8080 ) to start exploring! Advanced Installation For more control over the installation process, copy and paste the Docker Compose template into a docker-compose.yml file and customize it to your liking (be sure to replace any placeholders with your actual values). Then, run docker compose up -d to start the Command Center and its dependencies. Note: this method is recommended for advanced users only, as it requires familiarity with Docker and manual configuration before starting. How It Works N.O.M.A.D. is a management UI ("Command Center") and API that orchestrates a collection of containerized tools and resources via Docker . It handles installation, configuration, and updates for everything — so you don't hav
title_zh: Crosstalk-Solutions/project-nomad - N.O.M.A.D项目是一款独立离线的生存计算机，集成了关键工具、知识和人工智能，确保您随时随地获取信息并保持自主能力。
summary_zh: Crosstalk-Solutions在GitHub上开源了Project N.O.M.A.D.项目，其全称为“Node for Offline Media, Archives, and Data”。这是一个自包含、优先离线的知识与教育服务器，集成了关键工具、海量知识和AI能力，旨在让用户随时随地获取信息并保持自主能力。，该项目的核心是一个管理界面（称为“指挥中心”）和API，通过Docker协调一系列容器化的工具和资源。它自动处理所有组件的安装、配置和更新，用户无需手动管理。系统设计为通过浏览器访问，因此即使在没有桌面环境的服务器上也能运行，用户可通过本地主机（http://localhost:8080）或设备IP地址进行访问。，安装支持两种主要方式。对于基于Debian的系统（推荐Ubuntu），可通过终端执行一键安装脚本快速部署，此过程需要sudo权限。对于高级用户，项目提供了Docker Compose模板，允许进行更细致的自定义配置后再启动服务。，Project N.O.M.A.D.本质上是一个离线的综合知识库与工具平台，集成了AI功能，确保在无网络环境下也能访问重要信息和实用工具，适用于应急准备、远程教育或任何需要可靠离线资源的场景。其价值在于将分散的离线资源整合到一个统一、易于管理的系统中，为用户提供持久的、不依赖于互联网的知识支持和技术赋能。

title: supermemoryai/supermemory - Memory engine and app that is extremely fast, scalable. The Memory API for the AI era.
content: GitHub - supermemoryai/supermemory: Memory engine and app that is extremely fast, scalable. The Memory API for the AI era. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Docs · Quickstart · Dashboard · Discord Supermemory is the memory and context layer for AI. #1 on LongMemEval , LoCoMo , and ConvoMem — the three major benchmarks for AI memory. We are a research lab building the engine, plugins and tools around it. Your AI forgets everything between conversations. Supermemory fixes that. It automatically learns from conversations, extracts facts, builds user profiles, handles knowledge updates and contradictions, forgets expired information, and delivers the right context at the right time. Full RAG, connectors, file processing — the entire context stack, one system. 🧠 Memory Extracts facts from conversations. Handles temporal changes, contradictions, and automatic forgetting. 👤 User Profiles Auto-maintained user context — stable facts + recent activity. One call, ~50ms. 🔍 Hybrid Search RAG + Memory in a single query. Knowledge base docs and personalized context together. 🔌 Connectors Google Drive · Gmail · Notion · OneDrive · GitHub — auto-sync with real-time webhooks. 📄 Multi-modal Extractors PDFs, images (OCR), videos (transcription), code (AST-aware chunking). Upload and it works. All of this is in our single memory structure and ontology. Use Supermemory 🧑‍💻 I use AI tools Build your own personal supermemory by using our app. Builds persistent memory graph across every conversation . Your AI remembers your preferences, projects, past discussions — and gets smarter over time. → Jump to User setup 🔧 I'm building AI products Add memory, RAG, user profiles, and connectors to your agents and apps with a single API . No vector DB config. No embedding pipelines. No chunking strategies. → Jump to developer quickstart Give your AI memory The Supermemory App, browser extension, plugins and MCP server gives any compatible AI assistant persistent memory. One install, and your AI remembers you. The app You can use supermemory without any code, by using our co
title_zh: supermemoryai/supermemory - 极速、可扩展的记忆引擎与应用，AI时代的内存API。
summary_zh: supermemoryai/supermemory 是一个专为AI时代设计的高速、可扩展的记忆引擎与应用，被誉为“AI时代的记忆API”。该项目在LongMemEval、LoCoMo和ConvoMem三大AI记忆基准测试中均排名第一，旨在解决AI在对话间遗忘信息的问题，为其提供持久化的记忆与上下文层。，其核心功能是作为一个综合的记忆与上下文引擎，能够自动从对话中学习、提取事实、构建并维护用户画像、处理知识更新与矛盾，并适时遗忘过期信息，在正确的时间提供正确的上下文。系统整合了完整的RAG（检索增强生成）能力、多种连接器以及文件处理功能，构成了一个统一的全栈上下文系统。，关键技术特性包括：1. 记忆提取：从对话中提取事实，并能处理时序变化、矛盾信息及自动遗忘。2. 用户画像：自动维护包含稳定事实与近期活动的用户上下文，单次调用延迟约50毫秒。3. 混合搜索：在单一查询中结合RAG与记忆检索，将知识库文档与个性化上下文一并返回。4. 多平台连接器：支持自动同步Google Drive、Gmail、Notion、OneDrive、GitHub等平台，并配备实时Webhook。5. 多模态提取器：支持处理PDF、图像（OCR）、视频（转录）和代码（基于AST的智能分块），上传即可工作。所有这些功能都集成在其单一的记忆结构与本体论中。，项目提供两种主要使用方式：对于普通用户，可通过其应用程序、浏览器扩展、插件或MCP服务器，为兼容的AI助手赋予持久记忆，无需编写代码即可构建跨越所有对话的个人记忆图谱，使AI能记住用户偏好、项目与过往讨论。对于开发者，可通过单一API轻松为智能体（Agent）和应用添加记忆、RAG、用户画像和连接器功能，无需自行配置向量数据库、嵌入管道或分块策略。

title: usestrix/strix - Open-source AI hackers to find and fix your app’s vulnerabilities.
content: GitHub - usestrix/strix: Open-source AI hackers to find and fix your app’s vulnerabilities. · GitHub Skip to content You signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert usestrix / strix Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 2.3k Star 21.4k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 346 Commits 346 Commits .github .github benchmarks benchmarks containers containers docs docs scripts scripts strix strix tests tests .gitignore .gitignore .pre-commit-config.yaml .pre-commit-config.yaml CONTRIBUTING.md CONTRIBUTING.md LICENSE LICENSE Makefile Makefile README.md README.md poetry.lock poetry.lock pyproject.toml pyproject.toml strix.spec strix.spec View all files Repository files navigation Strix Open-source AI hackers to find and fix your app’s vulnerabilities. Tip New! Strix integrates seamlessly with GitHub Actions and CI/CD pipelines. Automatically scan for vulnerabilities on every pull request and block insecure code before it reaches production! Strix Overview Strix are autonomous AI agents that act just like real hackers - they run your code dynamically, find vulnerabilities, and validate them through actual proof-of-concepts. Built for developers and security teams who need fast, accurate security testing without the overhead of manual pentesting or the false positives of static analysis tools. Key Capabilities: Full hacker toolkit out of the box Teams of agents that collaborate and scale Real validation with PoCs, not false positives Developer‑first CLI with actionable reports Auto‑fix & reporting to accelerate remediation Use Cases Application Security Testing - Detect and validate critical vulnerabilities in your applications Rapid Penetration Testing - Get penetration tests done in hours, not weeks, with compliance reports Bug Bounty Automation - Automate bug bounty research and generate PoCs for faster reporting CI/CD Integration - Run tests in CI/CD to block vulnerabilities before reaching production 🚀 Quick Start Prerequisites: Docker (running) An LLM API key from any supported provider (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.) Installation & First Scan # Install Strix curl -sSL https://strix.ai/install | bash # Configure your AI provider export STRIX_LLM= " openai/gpt-5.4 " export LLM_API_KEY= " your-api-key " # Run your first security assessment strix --target ./app-directory Note First run automatically pulls the sandbox Docker image. Results are saved to strix_runs/<run-name> ☁️ Strix Platform Try the Strix full-stack security platform at app.strix.ai — sign up for free, connect your repos and domains, and launch a pentest in minutes. Validated findings with PoCs and reproduction steps One-click autofix as ready-to-m
title_zh: usestrix/strix - 开源AI黑客工具，助您发现并修复应用程序漏洞。
summary_zh: 开源项目Strix是一个利用AI黑客自动发现并修复应用程序漏洞的安全测试工具。该项目在GitHub上发布，核心定位是为开发者和安全团队提供快速、准确的安全测试，无需手动渗透测试的繁琐流程，也避免了静态分析工具常见的误报问题。，Strix的核心功能是部署自主的AI智能体，这些智能体模拟真实黑客的行为：动态运行用户代码，主动寻找漏洞，并通过实际的概念验证（PoC）来确认漏洞的有效性，从而确保发现的都是真实可被利用的安全问题，而非误报。，该工具提供开箱即用的完整黑客工具集，支持智能体团队协作与扩展。它配备了面向开发者的命令行界面（CLI），能生成包含可操作建议的详细报告，并支持自动修复功能，以加速漏洞的修复流程。Strix可无缝集成到GitHub Actions和CI/CD管道中，能够在每次拉取请求（Pull Request）时自动进行安全扫描，从而在问题代码进入生产环境之前进行拦截。，主要应用场景包括：应用程序安全测试（检测并验证关键漏洞）、快速渗透测试（在数小时内生成包含合规报告的测试结果，而非数周）、漏洞赏金自动化（自动进行漏洞赏金研究并生成PoC报告）以及CI/CD集成。，使用Strix需要两个前提条件：运行中的Docker环境，以及一个来自支持提供商（如OpenAI、Anthropic、Google等）的LLM API密钥。用户可以通过简单的安装脚本快速开始首次安全评估。此外，项目还提供了云端全栈安全平台（app.strix.ai），允许用户免费注册，连接代码仓库和域名，并在几分钟内启动渗透测试，该平台同样提供带PoC的已验证结果、复现步骤以及一键自动修复功能。

title: I tried to prove I'm not AI. My aunt wasn't convinced
content: I tried to prove I'm not AI. My aunt wasn't convinced Skip to content Home News US & Canada UK UK Politics England N. Ireland N. Ireland Politics Scotland Scotland Politics Wales Wales Politics Africa Asia China India Australia Europe Latin America Middle East In Pictures BBC InDepth BBC Verify Sport Business World of Business Technology of Business NYSE Opening Bell Technology Watch Documentaries Artificial Intelligence AI v the Mind Health Watch Documentaries Culture Watch Documentaries Film & TV Music Art & Design Style Books Entertainment News Arts Watch Documentaries Arts in Motion Travel Watch Documentaries Destinations Africa Antarctica Asia Australia and Pacific Caribbean & Bermuda Central America Europe Middle East North America South America World’s Table Culture & Experiences Adventures The SpeciaList Earth Watch Documentaries Science Natural Wonders Climate Solutions Sustainable Business Green Living Audio Podcast Categories Radio Audio FAQs Video Watch Documentaries BBC Maestro Discover the World Live Live News Live Sport Documentaries Home News Sport Business Technology Health Culture Arts Travel Earth Audio Video Live Documentaries Weather Newsletters Watch Live I tried to prove I'm not AI. My aunt wasn't convinced 3 hours ago Share Save Thomas Germain Share Save Serenity Strull/ Madeleine Jett/ BBC (Credit: Serenity Strull/ Madeleine Jett/ BBC) I asked experts if I'm real. Bad news. Even my aunt wasn't sure if I was a deepfake. AI is so convincing that a sitting prime minister struggled to prove he's alive. You might be next. I called up my aunt Eleanor a few days ago and asked her to help with an experiment. "It's for an article," I said. I had explained I was going to call her back and she'd either be talking to the real me or an AI deepfake. Could someone who's known me my whole life tell the difference? At first, my aunt wasn't buying that any AI was involved. "Well, it sounds like you," she said. "I think a real person uses a lot more inflection than I would expect an AI-generated voice to use." That might be true, I told her, but AI is getting pretty advanced. There was a long pause. "I was like 90% sure," she said, hesitating. "But that sounded more artificial." When we talk about deepfakes, the typical concern is about you getting tricked. Rightly so. AI fakery has been used to scam people out of large sums of money , spread misinformation and even attempt to sway elections . But what if the shoe was on the other foot? What if someone accuses you of being a deepfake? How do you prove you're real? That's a question Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu had to ask himself this month. He posted a video where a trick of the light made it look like he might have a glitchy sixth finger on his right hand, once a clear giveaway of AI deepfakes. The internet exploded with rumours that Netanyahu had died in a missile strike and Israel was covering it up. Days later, the prime minister posted a follow up video from a coffee shop,
title_zh: 我试图证明自己不是AI，但我的阿姨并不信服。
summary_zh: 近日，一位记者进行了一项实验：他致电从小认识自己的姨妈，告知她随后可能接到由AI生成的深度伪造（deepfake）语音来电，请她辨别真伪。尽管姨妈最初自信地表示能从语调中听出“真人感”，但在记者提醒AI技术已十分先进后，她变得犹豫，坦言“现在听起来更假了”。这一实验折射出深度伪造技术带来的新困境：当AI伪造内容足以乱真，人们不仅可能被虚假信息欺骗，甚至可能反过来被质疑是“假人”，面临自证清白的难题。，以色列总理内塔尼亚胡近期便遭遇类似困扰。他在一段视频中因光线错觉导致右手看似出现“第六指”——这一特征常被视作AI深伪的破绽，随即引发网络谣言称其已在导弹袭击中身亡，政府试图掩盖真相。尽管他后续发布在咖啡店拍摄的视频以证存活，事件仍凸显了公众人物乃至普通人在AI时代可能面临的“真实性危机”。，专家指出，当前AI生成的音频、视频已高度逼真，传统依赖语音语调、画面细节的辨别方式逐渐失效。深度伪造技术此前多被用于诈骗、传播虚假信息甚至干预选举，而如今“反被指认为深伪”成为新的风险点。这意味着，在技术快速演进的同时，社会需要建立更有效的身份验证机制与信息溯源体系，以应对信任缺失的挑战。如何证明“我是我”，或许将成为AI普及时代每个人必须思考的问题。

title: Is anybody else bored of talking about AI?
content: Is anybody else bored of talking about AI? Email JakeWritesCode LinkedIn About me Dark Mode At serious risk of sounding like a heretic here, but I’m kinda bored of talking about AI. I get it, AI is incredible. I use it every day, it’s completely changed my workflow. I recently started a new role in a tricky domain working at web scale (hey, remember web scale?) and it’s allowed me to go from 0-1 in terms of productivity in a matter of weeks. With that being said, it’s all starting to feel a bit… routine. I’m not here to argue that the pace of change has been incredible, but on a day-to-day basis I’ve sorta ran out of things to talk about. What makes this worse is it’s completely taken over mindshare across my section of the internet. Hacker News, my favourite haunt, used to be full of interesting projects and problems being solved but this seems to have devolved into three different people’s (almost identical) Claude code workflow and yet another post about how you got OpenClaw to stroke your cat and play video games so you had way more time to… configure AI tooling. This all feels a little self-fulfilling. Kagi small web is another great example of this effect. Here’s a challenge, open it up and press the ‘next’ button 20 times. What percentage of posts are AI related? Before you write me off as ‘old man yells at cloud’, understand where I’m coming from. In the good old days (2023), before we called anybody who could open a Claude code terminal an ‘AI engineer’, being a ‘Product Engineer’ was the hot new term. The idea was that engineers should move away from obsessing over code to obsessing over the product value they were delivering. I loved this, it made loads of sense to me, but we seem to have regressed. It’s no longer the code we’re obsessing over, it’s the overgrown auto-complete we’ve developed to make the easiest part of being an engineer easier. It’s like if I went onto the woodworking subreddit and they’d all stopped showing pictures of the tables they’d created and just started posting about the hammer they were using. But they were all using basically the same hammer in the same way, so they were just screaming the same shit at each other at the top of their voices. What makes this worse, is our bosses have bought into it this time too. My managers never cared much about database technologies, IDE’s or javascript frameworks; they just wanted the feature so they could sell it. Management seems to have stepped firmly and somewhat haphazardly into the implementation detail now. I reckon most of us have got some sort of company initiative to ‘use more AI’ in our objectives this year. Management’s involvement in the SDLC has always been a thing, DORA metrics have been around for a while. But historically, it’s always been about the outputs. Faster deploys, time to respond. Now we’re measuring the number of tokens used per-dev, which is no more useful than lines of code ever was. I guess what I’m saying, other than just having a general w
title_zh: 还有人厌倦谈论人工智能吗？
summary_zh: 还有人厌倦谈论AI吗？尽管听起来可能像异端邪说，但我确实有点厌倦谈论AI了。我理解AI非常了不起，每天使用它彻底改变了我的工作流程。最近我在一个棘手领域从事网络规模的新工作，AI让我在几周内实现了从零到一的生产力飞跃。然而，这一切开始让人感到有些……例行公事。我并不否认技术变革的速度惊人，但在日常层面，我已经没什么新鲜话题可讨论了。，更糟糕的是，AI完全占据了我所在网络社区的注意力。我曾最常访问的Hacker News过去充满有趣的项目和解决方案，如今却沦为三四个用户（几乎雷同）的Claude代码工作流程展示，或是关于如何用OpenClaw撸猫打游戏以便腾出时间配置AI工具的重复内容。这种自我循环的现象在Kagi小网络社区尤为明显——尝试连续点击20次“下一页”，会发现多大比例的内容与AI相关？，需要澄清的是，我并非守旧派。在2023年的“美好旧时光”，当“产品工程师”还是热门新概念时，我们倡导工程师应聚焦产品价值而非代码本身。但如今我们似乎倒退了： obsession的对象从代码变成了过度生长的自动补全工具，这就像木工论坛不再展示成品桌子，而是所有人用相同方式炫耀同一把锤子。，更严重的是，管理层这次也深陷其中。过去管理者只关心可销售的功能，对数据库技术、IDE或JavaScript框架并不在意，如今却开始混乱地介入实施细节。今年多数人的工作目标中都被塞入了“更多使用AI”的指标，就像过去用代码行数衡量效率一样，现在开始统计每个开发者的token使用量——这两种度量方式同样缺乏实际意义。，问题的核心在于，我们过度关注工具本身而忽视了创造价值。当技术讨论沦为重复的工具炫耀，当创新被简化为参数配置竞赛，整个领域就陷入了自我指涉的循环。或许我们需要回归本质：AI应该是解决问题的桥梁，而非问题本身。

title: So where are all the AI apps?
content: So where are all the AI apps? – Answer.AI Fans of vibecoding and agentic tools say they are 2x as productive, 10x as productive – maybe 100x as productive! Someone built an entire web browser from scratch . Amazing! So, skeptics reasonably ask, where are all the apps? If AI users are becoming (let’s be conservative) merely 2x more productive, then where do we look to see 2x more software being produced? Such questions all start from the assumption that the world wants more software, so that if software has gotten cheaper to make then people will make more of it. So if you agree with that assumption, then where is the new software surplus, what we might call the “AI effect”? We’ll look at PyPI, the central repository for Python packages. It’s large, public, and consistently measured, so we should expect to see some AI effect there. Counting packages There it is, see it? The release of ChatGPT. Does it look like an epochal revolution of software productivity on the upper chart? No. There are a few spikes in the lower chart showing new packages/month, in what you might call the “AI era” of 2020 onward. But those reflect spam and malware floods, not genuine package creation. 1 Two-panel chart showing PyPI total packages growing exponentially to 800k and new packages per month fluctuating around 5-15k, with ChatGPT release marked showing no obvious inflection point This is curious. If AI is making software engineers more productive, why aren’t they producing more software? Counting updates But, you might say, package creation is not the right measure. Anyone can create and upload a “package” which is nothing but a hello world demo. This is always easier than creating something durable which people actually use. We want to look at “real” packages, packages which are actually downloaded, used, and maintained over time. Okay, so let’s consider a different chart. We start by gathering the 15,000 most downloaded Python packages on PyPI in December 2025. 2 Then we split the packages into cohorts based on their birth-year, and for each cohort we plot their median release frequency over time. 3 This seems like a reasonable proxy measure of the production of real, actively-used software. To show one cohort’s release frequency over time, we draw a line. So in the chart below, every line starts with a point showing the number of update releases within the first 12 months of the life of a package born in that year. The line proceeds as the package ages. So what do we see? Do packages get updated more frequently after the advent of ChatGPT? Well … sort of? We clearly see that packages born after ChatGPT were updated more frequently within their first year (13 releases/year) than packages born back in 2014 (6 releases/year). This is seen in the fact that the cohort life lines start higher over time. But this looks like it’s continuing a trend which starts too early to be attributed to an AI productivity boost. First-year release frequency started increasing in 2019
summary_zh: AI应用究竟在哪里？——来自Answer.AI的思考，当前AI领域充满乐观论调，支持者声称AI编程工具能将开发效率提升2倍、10倍甚至100倍，例如有人仅用AI就从头构建了完整的网页浏览器。然而质疑者提出一个合理问题：如果AI真能大幅提升效率，为何我们没有看到软件产出量的显著增长？这背后隐含着一个假设：如果软件开发成本降低，市场将自然产生更多软件。那么所谓的“AI效应”带来的软件盈余究竟体现在何处？，为探究这一问题，我们分析了Python官方软件仓库PyPI的数据。PyPI拥有超过80万个软件包，每月新增约5千至1.5万个。值得注意的是，2022年ChatGPT发布后，该平台并未出现明显的增长拐点。虽然2020年进入“AI时代”后每月新增包数量出现过几次峰值，但经分析这些主要来自垃圾邮件和恶意软件的泛滥，而非真实的软件创作。，若仅统计新包数量不够准确，我们进一步考察了“真实软件包”的维护活跃度。通过分析PyPI在2025年12月下载量最高的1.5万个软件包，按出生年份分组并追踪其中位更新频率，发现2022年后诞生的软件包在第一年更新频率确实更高（年均13次），显著高于2014年出生的包（年均6次）。但这一增长趋势实际始于2019年，早于ChatGPT的发布，说明更新频率的提升可能源于更早的技术演进，而非完全归功于AI生产力革命。，综合来看，尽管AI工具在理论上能极大提升开发效率，但这种提升尚未在宏观的软件产出指标上形成显著突破。这可能意味着：AI带来的效率增益被转化为更复杂的软件功能、更高的代码质量或更快的迭代周期，而非单纯的数量增长；或者AI工具目前仍主要应用于现有项目的优化而非全新软件的创造。真正的“AI应用爆发”或许需要更长时间才能体现在生态系统层面的数据中。
